title: The First Odd-Odd Open Case for BSHM(8rs,4s,4s,0)
micro_paper_assessment: Strong theorem-facing reduction proved, but the exact existence question remains open because the reduced 60-row block-balanced orthogonal completion problem was not closed.
family_affinity: High; the Gram-to-clique reduction scales across BSHM(n,ell,ell,0), and here it forces twelve six-column classes in the Type 2 family.
generalization_signal: The clique-union and block-balance reduction scales, but the global orthogonal completion problem remains the hard instance-sensitive residue.
candidate_theorem_slice: BSHM(72,12,12,0) exists if and only if there is a 60x72 orthogonal +/-1 completion whose rows are 3-balanced on each of twelve six-column blocks forced by the selected rows.
next_action: Verify the source-faithful reduction and then attack or obstruct the reduced 60-row block-balanced completion problem without broadening beyond this micro-paper lane.
